title: Test: Worker route integration (full HTTP flows)
close_reason: worker-routes.test.ts: 122 tests passing

title: Fix 48 failing existing tests
close_reason: All 740 tests passing, 0 failures

title: Route GitHub webhook endpoint for claim-by-commit flow
description: GitHubApp class has full implementation (webhook signature verification, JWT generation, claim token extraction) but NO route receives webhooks. Need: POST /webhooks/github route in worker, verify signature, call GitHubApp.handlePush(), handle claim result. Without this, the Connect→Operate→Claim flow is broken.
close_reason: Already implemented - POST /webhook/github route exists with HMAC verification and claim handling

title: Implement scoped API keys
description: API keys currently grant full identity permissions with no scoping. When a key is created via /api/keys, it is associated with an identity but cannot be restricted to specific entities, operations, or tenants. Need: scope claim on API key records, tenant-scoped keys, operation-scoped keys, checked by requireAuth() middleware.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement CLI (login, logout, whoami, token, status)
close_reason: CLI implemented: login, logout, whoami, token, status — 49 tests

title: Fix broken .do domain CORS origin regex
description: In src/csrf/index.ts line 55, the regex /^https?:\/\/([a-z0-9-]+\.)*\.do$/ requires a literal dot before 'do', so it matches https://.do (invalid) but NOT https://oauth.do or https://events.do. No .do domain passes the CORS origin check, causing silent auth failures for cross-origin requests from .do domains. Fix: change to /^https?:\/\/([a-z0-9-]+\.)*[a-z0-9-]+\.do$/ or enumerate specific .do domains.
close_reason: Fixed in e1fb573 — isSafeRedirectUrl + .do CORS regex

title: Test: IdentityDO (provision, claim, sessions, API keys, rate limits)
close_reason: identity-do.test.ts: 149 tests passing

title: Delete unused createOAuth21Server factory chain (endpoints/routes/helpers/utils + test)
description: Investigation confirmed: zero non-test consumers of createOAuth21Server. Imports limited to src/sdk/oauth/{index,server,helpers}.ts (internal) + test/oauth-server.test.ts. The deployed worker/routes/oauth.ts uses OAuthProvider (the class) directly. Factory chain was migrated from @dotdo/oauth in eaf83d7 (Feb 22 2026) for an oauth.do cutover that never happened — oauth.do still imports from @dotdo/oauth. Plan: delete src/sdk/oauth/{server.ts, helpers.ts, endpoints/*, routes/*, utils/*}, test/oauth-server.test.ts; remove re-exports from src/sdk/oauth/index.ts. Keep OAuthProvider class (deployed surface), provider.ts (1502 LOC), and types.ts/pkce.ts/jwt-verify.ts/consent.ts/guards.ts/dev.ts/stripe.ts/clients.ts. Verify storage.ts retention based on clients.ts/dev.ts usage. Net delete: ~3000 LOC. Zero regression risk to OAuth surface.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement automated RSA key rotation
description: No automated key rotation mechanism exists. SigningKeyManager supports multiple keys (for rotation) but there is no cron job or scheduled handler to rotate keys. Key rotation is manual. Need: (1) scheduled handler (Cloudflare Cron Trigger) to rotate keys on cadence (e.g. every 90 days), (2) during rotation both old and new keys in JWKS for overlap period, (3) CI/CD step to rebuild and deploy the CDN auth snippet after rotation.
close_reason: Already shipped; verified in current main branch (commits dad8cb4, 87c990c, ab76502, 159ec1e, f3913f2)

title: OneCLI SecretBackend trait — upstream PR + id.org.ai reference impl (fork+bundle as fallback)
description: OneCLI's gateway is hard-wired to sqlx::PgPool — no remote-backend HTTPS API today. Plan: (1) draft SecretBackend trait + RFC, ship PR upstream to github.com/onecli/onecli; (2) implement reference backend in id.org.ai backed by Vault (#6). Fallback if upstream rejects: fork the gateway, bundle into the id.org.ai (or org.ai) CLI as 'org.ai proxy' via npm postinstall per-platform binaries or TS rewrite. Spec work can start in parallel with #3-#6.
close_reason: PARKED — long lead time, upstream-PR-driven. Confirmed (memory project_positioning.md): OneCLI gateway has no remote-backend protocol today; sqlx::PgPool hardcoded against 5 fixed tables. Three execution paths in increasing effort: (A) speak Postgres wire protocol vs OneCLI's schema (brittle, no upstream coordination), (B) fork the gateway and bundle the binary into id.org.ai/org.ai CLI as 'org.ai proxy' (requires Rust cross-compilation in npm postinstall OR TS rewrite), (C) propose a SecretBackend trait + RFC + PR upstream to github.com/onecli/onecli (slowest, but standard-shaped — id.org.ai becomes spec author, not implementer). Recommended: (C) first, with (B) as fallback if upstream rejects. Tenant-vault SDK from id-z7d (b04e936) is the load-bearing reference impl — putTenantSecret/getTenantSecretValue/listTenantSecrets/deleteTenantSecret already shipped with WorkOS Vault backing. Handoff to a session that can take 1-2 weeks of upstream PR iteration.

title: Build GitHub App webhook handler for claim-by-commit
close_reason: All implemented: GitHub App webhook, GitHub Action, MCP explore/try/search/fetch/do tools, Ed25519 keypairs with DID format

title: Implement OAuth 2.1 provider with PKCE and Device Flow
close_reason: Full OAuth 2.1 provider implemented: PKCE, Device Flow, refresh token rotation, DCR, introspection, revocation (1354 lines)

title: Deprecate org.ai package — thin re-export of id.org.ai
close_reason: org.ai is NOT being deprecated — it re-exports id.org.ai plus ~30 other .org.ai primitives

title: Replace catch(err: any) with catch(err: unknown)
description: Multiple catch blocks use 'any' type (worker/index.ts lines 808, 1154) and access err.message without guards. If err is not an Error (e.g. a string throw), accessing .message returns undefined. Fix: use catch(err: unknown) with err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err).
close_reason: Already shipped; verified in current main branch (commits dad8cb4, 87c990c, ab76502, 159ec1e, f3913f2)

title: Fix mcpDo audit leak — route entity-mutation events through AuditService
description: src/server/do/Identity.ts mcpDo writes domain events directly via ctx.storage.put('event:...') and never calls auditService.log. Combined with writeAuditEvent RPC which lets worker routes write audit records by raw key bypassing the service. Both are leaky escape hatches. Plan: route mcpDo events through AuditService, delete writeAuditEvent RPC, make AuditService.log the only writer. ~30 LOC change.
close_reason: Closed

title: Cache SigningKeyManager at module level
description: In worker/index.ts, own JWT verification creates a new SigningKeyManager per request and calls oauthStub.oauthStorageOp() to load keys from the DO. The manager has an in-memory cache (loaded flag) but a new instance is created each time across requests. Every JWT verification hits IdentityDO storage at least once per request. Fix: cache the SigningKeyManager or its JWKS data at module level, similar to auth-verifier's jwksCache pattern.
close_reason: Already shipped; verified in current main branch (commits dad8cb4, 87c990c, ab76502, 159ec1e, f3913f2)

title: Replace flat entity storage with queryable format for MCP tools
description: MCP do handler stores entities as flat key-value: entity:{id}:{type}:{id}. Cannot implement search() or fetch() MCP tools — no filtering by type/stage/fields. Need: migrate to D1 or structured DO storage that supports queries. Currently search tool returns stubs, fetch tool cannot filter.
close_reason: Implemented: queryable DO storage with secondary indexes

title: Implement MCP auth with progressive capability tiers (L0-L3)
close_reason: MCPAuth implemented with L0-L3 progressive capability tiers as part of id-p5u

title: Test: Cookie auth (set, read, expiry, clear)
close_reason: cookie-auth.test.ts: 93 tests passing

title: Fix open redirect in login/logout flows
description: Login continue URL and logout return_url are used in HTTP Location headers without validation. An attacker can craft https://auth.headless.ly/login?continue=https://evil.com to redirect users to malicious sites after authentication. The logout endpoint has no CSRF protection and takes redirect URL directly from query string. Fix: validate redirect URLs against isAllowedOrigin() or only allow relative paths (starting with /). Files: worker/index.ts lines 752, 856, 874.
close_reason: Fixed in e1fb573 — isSafeRedirectUrl + .do CORS regex

title: AAP wire surface (Agent Auth Protocol v1.0) — discovery + agent + capability endpoints
description: Implement Agent Auth Protocol v1.0-draft from agent-auth-protocol.com: GET /.well-known/agent-configuration, POST /agent/register, GET /agent/status, POST /agent/{revoke,reactivate,rotate-key,request-capability,introspect}, POST /host/{revoke,rotate-key}, POST /capability/{list,describe,execute}. Two JWT typ values: host+jwt and agent+jwt, EdDSA over Ed25519. List on agent-auth.directory once shipping. Depends on #3.
close_reason: Phase 1 shipped (6d124bc): discovery doc + agent lifecycle endpoints (register/status/revoke/reactivate) over existing auth (session/API key). 17 route tests pass. Discovery doc declares conformance notes for AAP's host+jwt + capability/* endpoints as null — those are phase 2. Phase 2 (host+jwt issuance + verification, agent+jwt signature verification with existing crypto/keys.ts utilities, capability/execute paired with id-lkj FGA migration) deferred to a follow-up; current surface is enough to list on agent-auth.directory and to accept AAP-shaped agent registrations from the better-auth client SDK.

title: better-auth / Auth.js / NextAuth — supported-provider listings
description: Position id.org.ai as a drop-in OIDC provider that better-auth, Auth.js, Lucia, NextAuth list alongside Auth0/WorkOS-AuthKit. Each has a supported-provider PR process. Conformance: validate OIDC discovery fields, JWKS rotation, PKCE-only, refresh-token shape, branded login button. Blocked on #1 (OAuth surface stabilises on createOAuth21Server) and #3 (host/agent shape settled).
close_reason: PARKED — external PR work, not internal code. Conformance prerequisites done: id-2zc shipped (single OAuth provider on createOAuth21Server-shaped surface — actually OAuthProvider class), id-ax7 shipped (host/agent shape stable). Next: (1) audit OIDC discovery doc fields for better-auth/Auth.js conformance (refresh_token shape, PKCE-only, JWKS rotation, branded login button assets), (2) submit supported-provider PRs to github.com/better-auth/better-auth, github.com/nextauthjs/next-auth, github.com/lucia-auth/lucia. Each repo has a 'Adding a new provider' guide. Estimated: 2-3 days of doc + PR review iteration. Handoff: this is marketing-leverage work, not architectural; no engineering blockers.

title: Test: OAuth 2.1 provider (all grant types, PKCE, device flow)
close_reason: oauth-provider.test.ts: 110 tests passing

title: Host/agent schema split — promote agent_keys to first-class agents under host identity
description: AAP requires distinct host (persistent client env) and agent (per-session actor) identities, where revoking an agent does not affect the host or sibling agents. Today IdentityType is flat: 'human' | 'agent' | 'service' with agent_keys as a per-identity credential. Plan: promote agent to first-class entity under a host identity; touch Identity schema, audit actor field, AuthBroker identityId, MCPAuth, rate-limiter key. Prerequisite for AAP (#4) and FGA migration (#5).
notes: Design spec: docs/specs/2026-05-05-tenant-agent-split-design.md. D1-D10 + Vault + 4 resolved Qs locked. Ready for execution session.
close_reason: Schema split shipped across 6 phases (1, 3, 4a, 4b, 5, 7) — IdentityType rename, first-class Agent entity under Tenant, IdentityDO RPC surface, AuthBroker dispatch on agent_* principals, mcpDo tenant ownership, legacy AgentKeyService removed. ~1,000 LOC net add (incl. service + tests) - 800 LOC net delete (legacy cleanup). 71 test files / 2119 tests pass. Spec: docs/specs/2026-05-05-tenant-agent-split-design.md.

title: Add CSRF protection and audit logging
description: No CSRF tokens on provision/claim endpoints, CORS is origin:* (overly permissive), no origin validation. Also zero audit logging — no logging of identity creation, claim events, key registration, rate limit breaches, or failed auth attempts. Need: CSRF tokens on OAuth flows, origin validation, audit log for all sensitive operations.
close_reason: Implemented: standard error format, CSRF + audit logging

title: Remove unused D1 database config or wire Drizzle ORM
description: D1 database 'id-org-ai' is declared in wrangler.jsonc but NEVER used. Drizzle ORM schema is defined in src/db/schema.ts but never instantiated. All data lives in DO storage. Either: (A) migrate session/entity data to D1 for queryable storage, or (B) remove D1 config and Drizzle schema to eliminate dead code.
close_reason: Implemented in hardening wave

title: Add Ed25519 agent keypair support and DID format
close_reason: All implemented: GitHub App webhook, GitHub Action, MCP explore/try/search/fetch/do tools, Ed25519 keypairs with DID format

title: Fix CSRF timingSafeEqual length leak
description: In src/csrf/index.ts lines 303-310, timingSafeEqual returns false immediately when lengths differ, leaking whether lengths match. Since CSRF tokens are always 64 hex chars the practical risk is low. Fix: use crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual on Workers or hash both values first.
close_reason: Already shipped; verified in current main branch (commits dad8cb4, 87c990c, ab76502, 159ec1e, f3913f2)

title: Move id.org.ai DNS to Cloudflare
description: id.org.ai DNS currently points to Vercel (marketing site), not the Cloudflare auth worker. This means: (1) OIDC discovery at https://id.org.ai/.well-known/openid-configuration returns nothing useful, (2) standard OIDC clients expecting id.org.ai as issuer fail discovery, (3) JWT iss claim cannot be validated by libraries that auto-discover JWKS. Worker is reachable via auth.headless.ly as workaround. Moving DNS to Cloudflare is a correctness issue for OIDC compliance.
close_reason: DNS already moved. id.org.ai resolves to Cloudflare nameservers (dayana/sean.ns.cloudflare.com) and Cloudflare IPs. /.well-known/openid-configuration returns 200 with the full OIDC discovery doc; /.well-known/jwks.json returns 200 with the active signing key. Worker route 'id.org.ai/*' configured in worker/wrangler.jsonc:39.

title: Wire OAuth 2.1 provider into worker routing
description: 800-line OAuth 2.1 provider exists in src/oauth/provider.ts but is NOT wired into the worker. getOAuthProvider(c) is called but never defined — runtime crash on OIDC Discovery. Need: define getOAuthProvider(), route /oauth/authorize, /oauth/token, /oauth/device, /oauth/register endpoints, implement token response with JWT signing.
close_reason: Already implemented - OAuth 2.1 provider fully wired with all routes in worker/index.ts

title: Build GitHub Action (dot-org-ai/id@v1) for claim confirmation
close_reason: All implemented: GitHub App webhook, GitHub Action, MCP explore/try/search/fetch/do tools, Ed25519 keypairs with DID format

title: Shard IdentityDO by tenant instead of global singleton
description: All requests funnel through idFromName('global') — single DO instance. Cloudflare DOs have CPU/request-rate limits. Unscalable past ~1000 concurrent agents, multi-tenancy broken. Fix: use idFromName(tenantId ?? identityId) to shard by tenant. Every identity/tenant gets its own DO.
close_reason: Implemented in hardening wave

title: Standardize error response format across all endpoints
description: Three different error formats: {error:'unauthorized'}, {error:'unauthorized',message:'...'}, {error_description:'...'}. Client code cannot standardize error handling. Pick one format (OAuth error_description or plain error), define error code constants, validate all endpoints return consistent shape.
close_reason: Implemented: standard error format, CSRF + audit logging

title: Replace LEVEL_SCOPES ladder with WorkOS FGA tuple checks
description: Today id.org.ai gates on flat scope strings via LEVEL_SCOPES[L0..L4] in src/sdk/mcp/auth.ts and worker/middleware/require-scope.ts. With WorkOS FGA (GA Feb 17 2026, Warrant-based ReBAC), agents and hosts are subjects, capabilities are tuples, Check API replaces the hardcoded ladder. Plan: wire workos.fga.check into AuthBroker, define tuple namespace for id.org.ai resources, migrate requireScope/requireAllScopes to FGA-backed checks. Depends on #3.
close_reason: PARKED — needs dedicated session. Substantial migration touching every authz site (MCPAuth.LEVEL_SCOPES, requireScope/requireAllScopes middleware, AuthBroker.check + gate). Foundations in place: src/sdk/workos/fga.ts has the WorkOS FGA primitives (createWarrant/checkPermission/batchCheck/etc) — just needs wiring into the broker. Recommended approach: (1) add async FGA path to AuthBroker.gate() that triggers on need.resource being set, (2) project AAP agent capabilities (Agent.capabilities[] from id-ax7) into FGA tuples on agent registration, (3) migrate routes to FGA checks one at a time (per-route adoption pattern, not big-bang). Greenfield → no production-LEVEL_SCOPES users to migrate, so this can land cleanly. Spec hints in docs/specs/2026-05-05-tenant-agent-split-design.md D10. Handoff to next id.org.ai dev session.

title: Align isAdmin role check with auth-verifier
description: id.org.ai AuthService.isAdmin() checks ['admin', 'superadmin'] (worker/index.ts line 434), while auth-verifier checks only ['admin']. A superadmin user is recognized as admin by id.org.ai but NOT by auth-verifier. Both should check the same role set. Fix in both repos.
close_reason: Already shipped; verified in current main branch (commits dad8cb4, 87c990c, ab76502, 159ec1e, f3913f2)

close_reason: Anonymous provisioning, MCPAuth, ClaimService, IdentityDO endpoints all implemented

title: Fix test suite execution (vitest not found)
description: 4 test files exist (~97 tests per git log) covering MCP auth, MCP tools, crypto keys, and claim service. But pnpm test fails with 'vitest: command not found' because dependencies aren't installed. Fix: ensure pnpm install works in workspace context, add to CI, fix any test failures.
close_reason: Implemented in hardening wave

title: Implement id_token issuance for OIDC compliance
description: Server metadata advertises id_token_signing_alg_values_supported but no actual id_token generation exists in the token endpoint. Token endpoint returns access_token and refresh_token but not id_token. Infrastructure exists (JWKS, SigningKeyManager, nonce support) — just needs actual id_token generation in the code grant exchange. This is the single biggest OIDC compliance gap.
close_reason: id_token issuance implemented in src/sdk/oauth/provider.ts:1230-1262 — signs with current key, includes nonce/email/profile/tier/at_hash claims (OIDC Core 3.1.3.6 compliant)

title: Add org field to AuthUser type (parity with auth-verifier)
description: id.org.ai's AuthUser type (worker/index.ts line 126-135) is missing the deprecated 'org' field that auth-verifier populates. Consumers checking user.org get undefined when token was verified by id.org.ai but it works via auth-verifier. Either add org to id.org.ai AuthUser or migrate all consumers to use organizationId. Part of broader AuthUser consolidation effort.
close_reason: Closed

title: WorkOS Vault wrapper — tenant-keyed secret storage for agent downstream credentials
description: Wrap workos.vault.{createObject, readObject, updateObject, ...} with key_context = id.org.ai tenantId so agents store and retrieve downstream credentials (Stripe, GitHub, etc.) without us holding plaintext. Backbone for OneCLI backend (#7) and the auth-by-default story for digital-objects/ai-database/ai-functions. Independent; ships in parallel with #3-#5.
close_reason: SDK-level tenant-scoped Vault wrapper shipped (b04e936): putTenantSecret/getTenantSecretValue/listTenantSecrets/deleteTenantSecret with cross-tenant isolation by naming convention. 15 tests pass. HTTP routes + IdentityDO RPC deferred — those are consumed by id-9s0 (AAP wire surface) and id-4gd (OneCLI backend) when those land. The SDK functions are the load-bearing piece; OneCLI / AAP / digital-objects can import them directly today.
